mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals policy easing that lowers equity discount rates and improves risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when the policy path is declining.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month lookback, or the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening household income and demand, which raises recession risk and pressures equity multiples lower over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive outcomes occur in more than 60% of triggered cases over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST indicates improving consumer demand and housing activity, which supports earnings expectations and lifts SPX over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive when both series rise on a 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with steady PAYEMS reflects expanding real output and labor utilization, which improves revenue visibility and supports positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% over 2 months and PAYEMS rises
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases fail to show higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low inflation regime combined with very low policy rates suppresses discount rates and keeps financial conditions easy, which favors positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation, low-rate episodes do not have meaningfully higher 20-day SPX returns than other periods, or if the joint condition is not more predictive than either proxy separately.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently weak PAYEMS growth signals soft payroll income and weaker consumption, so S&P 500 returns over the next week are negative when payrolls contract.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines are not followed by negative average 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect reverses in more than one third of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 trend indicates improving aggregate demand and earnings capacity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with rising GDPC1 do not coincide with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship disappears after accounting for revision-safe vintages.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production weakens while unemployment is rising, the economy is entering a production-labor squeeze that typically compresses equity valuations and produces negative SPX returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 months and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration signal does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction is not stronger than the individual series effects in rolling out-of-sample tests.
